channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chip makers slumped early Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Google search ditches country-specific domains, directs all users to google.com
item.description: With search results now displaying localized content, Google will be redirecting all visits through google.com.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI: the ultimate check against organizational well-being
item.description: There’s nothing like a technological paradigm shift to test your organization’s health and ability to absorb change. Just like businesses had to embrace mobility and cloud computing or risk being left behind, they’re now having to find ways to use AI effectively.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cloud Next 2025: las innovaciones en IA de Google que los CIO deberían conocer
item.description: En Cloud Next 2025, Google anunció varias actualizaciones que podrían ayudar a los directores de sistemas de información (CIO, por sus siglas en inglés) a adoptar y escalar agentes, al tiempo que reducen la complejidad y los costes de integración.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FTC grills Zuckerberg over emails from Instagram, WhatsApp acquisitions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) grilled Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday over dozens of emails and messages he exchanged with other executives in the lead-up to the social media giant’s acquisitions of Instagram and WhatsApp.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Couldn’t install Windows 11 24H2 because of your wallpaper? Microsoft has finally lifted blocks on upgrades due to customization apps – with some catches
item.description: Never been offered the Windows 11 24H2 upgrade? You might see it now, if the problem was a third-party customization app on your PC.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk’s X faces EU probe over GDPR violations in AI training
item.description: Elon Musk’s X is facing a regulatory probe in Europe over its alleged use of public posts from EU users to train its Grok AI chatbot – an investigation that could set a precedent for how companies use publicly available data under the bloc’s privacy laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google's New Approach to Cloud Integration
item.description: Google's announcement at the Google Cloud Next 2025 conference in Las Vegas marks a significant shift in its approach to cloud integration, embracing collaboration and flexibility over exclusivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Measuring success in dataops, data governance, and data security
item.description: Back in 2006, British mathematician Clive Humby stated that data was the new oil. Like oil, data isn’t useful in its raw state and must be refined, processed, and distributed to deliver value.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intelligence at the edge opens up more risks: how unified SASE can solve it
item.description: In an increasingly mobile and modern workforce, smart technologies such as AI-driven edge solutions and the Internet of Things (IoT) can help enterprises improve productivity and efficiency—whether to address operational roadblocks or respond faster to market demands.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: US moves ahead on tariffs with investigations into computer chips and pharmaceuticals
item.description: The Trump administration has taken its next steps toward imposing more tariffs on key imports, launching investigations into imports of computer chips and products that contain them, chip making equipment and pharmaceuticals.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta faces potential breakup with start of antitrust trial
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission is finally having its day in court against Meta Platforms Inc., arguing that the company must be broken up for illegally monopolizing the social media market after buying Instagram and WhatsApp more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta vs. Federal Trade Commission trial begins Monday. What to know
item.description: The FTC alleges Meta violated competition laws when purchasing Instagram and WhatsApp, in 2012 and 2014 respectively.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google Cloud Next 2025: News and insights
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas is highlighting the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A multicloud experiment in agentic AI: Lessons learned
item.description: Recently I undertook a project to design and validate agentic AI architectures capable of operating autonomously across various public cloud providers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google unveils Firebase Studio for AI app development
item.description: Google is previewing Firebase Studio, a cloud-based agentic development environment designed to build, test, deploy, and run AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI
item.description: OMB’s new acquisition memo to buy AI details only four deadlines for agencies over the next year, but outlines a host of new concepts to follow. The post OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google’s BigQuery and Looker get agents to simplify analytics tasks
item.description: Google has added new agents to its BigQuery data warehouse and Looker business intelligence platform to help data practitioners automate and simplify analytics tasks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cutting-edge drone tech maps land and water with laser accuracy
item.description: New drone technology maps land and water with stunning accuracy, giving researchers and conservationists a new way to understand our planet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Waymo gets stuck in California Chick-fil-A drive-thru
item.description: Videos showed Chick-fil-A workers standing around the stalled vehicle, which had its hazard lights on.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation
